criteria_checked.criterion_ref: master §M7 acceptance — canonical (4)(5) + empty-allowed REJECT with fixtures (dmc-v1-runtime-upgrade.md:318-319, :134-141)
criteria_checked.note: Plan §Acceptance rows + test-worker-adversarial.sh design cover JWT/six oauth classes (oauth-cli-adapter.py:37-44 verified), rename/copy/binary/c-quoted/zero-path diffs, empty-allowed, plus ACCEPT positive controls; gaps re-verified against live bytes (worker-result-check.py:21-23,:26-34,:59; worker-context-guard.sh:19-21,:52-53 fail-open confirmed).

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: master §M7 acceptance — v0.3.3 contract suite green UNCHANGED (34/0/2, pinned stages)
criteria_checked.note: C1 required list (dmc-v0.3.3-verify.sh:66-68) exactly equals the plan's result floor; mktask (:36) satisfies the task floor; all three adapters stamp result.task_id and provider fields from the task (glm-api-adapter.py:186,199-201; oauth-cli-adapter.py:227,240-241; manual-import-adapter.py:210), so v0.3.3 success rows and pinned C5a stages survive the cross-checks.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: plan Goal/Acceptance — legacy aggregate 802/3/3 EXACT under selftest --all
criteria_checked.note: See blocker B1: the unconditional provider/type cross-check and the provider_target non-empty floor flip pinned ACCEPT rows in v0.2.1, v0.2.1.1 (~12 rows), and v0.2.3 V6 to FAIL.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: master §M7 acceptance — apply without chain refused
criteria_checked.note: apply-check REFUSES on a missing authorization and the plan states this definition explicitly (plan L266-272); honesty caveat on the invocation tier recorded as advisory A5.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: master §M7 acceptance — INSTALL_MANIFEST drift re-run clean post-M7
criteria_checked.note: ring0_lib_list/schema_list are dynamic (dmc-install.sh:60-62), hooks list is name-only (:37-39), so content-only validator edits do not drift and the two NEW files do — regen+commit then test-manifest-drift.sh byte-equality is exactly right; providers ARE shipped recursively (:307-312), validating the import-fail-closed mitigation. Host-side authorizations-directory gap recorded as advisory A3.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: .harness/schemas/worker-review.schema.md conformance (5-kind enum, 4 mandatory kinds, decision apply|reject, all four :37-39 negative controls, terminal-REJECT/new-task-id vs authorize overwrite refusal)
criteria_checked.note: Plan review-check/authorize semantics match the committed contract verbatim; re-dispatch-needs-new-task-id (schema :34) defuses the overwrite-refusal conflict; result_id fallback is workable (provider_metadata.invocation_id is a real field, WORKER_RESULT_SCHEMA.md:26). Registry-id divergence of the schema's illustrative reviewer_role recorded as advisory A1.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: protected-surface authorization + single-owner + allow-rows (master row L74, §M7 Not-edit)
criteria_checked.note: Both validator edits authorized by master L74/§M7; T012.1-.6 ownership is disjoint; frozen surfaces (M6 hooks, providers L92, installer, root WORKER_* schemas) correctly no-rowed. Master skills-row milestone tag (L75 says M5) relies on this plan's own gate — advisory A7.
`bin/dmc validate plan` returned VALID at the frozen bytes; grep for DMC-T012.[0-9] across .harness/ docs/ excluding this plan returned zero hits.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: rollback — pre-M7 fixture equals live validator bytes (plan L119-124, master :320-321)
criteria_checked.note: cmp clean this session for both tests/fixtures/hooks-v0.6.5/hooks files vs live; test-restore.sh (in the --all loop) compares against a pinned commit, not the live tree, so M7 edits cannot break it; test-rollback.sh is confirmed absent from run_m6_suite (bin/dmc:162).

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: manual-import dynamic-import API preservation (plan L57-58, L182-183, risk L429)
criteria_checked.note: See blocker B2: manual-import-adapter.py:85 also imports diff_paths; the plan pins only DISALLOWED.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: fixture-suite realism (mktemp scope.lock arming, porcelain guard, no live-repo writes)
criteria_checked.note: _m6common.sh arm_fixture (:173-184) proves the real `dmc run start`→scope-lock path in a disposable repo; porcelain before/after helpers exist in both M6/M8 commons; dmc-scope-lock.py --adjudicate LOCK PATH OP signature confirmed (:18). Bytecode-cache trap recorded as advisory A2.

__file__-relative resolution is cwd-independent; ../workers/providers/oauth-cli/ is the correct relative path from .claude/hooks/; oauth-cli-adapter.py module level is import-safe (no main execution, no guard-call circularity); find_token_material returns pattern labels only (:90-105).

blockers.statement: The unconditional task/provider cross-checks and the task-side floor contradict the plan's own '802/3/3 EXACT' goal and acceptance criterion: the committed task .harness/workers/tasks/mock-001.json declares provider_target.type='mock', provider='mock-local', but the glm adapter unconditionally stamps result.provider_metadata.provider_type='api_key', provider='glm-api' (glm-api-adapter.py:199-201), so the plan's cross-check ('any mismatch ⇒ REJECT', plan L217-220) flips pinned ACCEPT rows to FAIL in bin/lib/dmc-v0.2.1-verify.sh:18 and every ACCEPT row of bin/lib/dmc-v0.2.1.1-verify.sh (accept() at :19 defaults to mock-001; ~12 call sites). Additionally the task floor 'provider_target.provider non-empty ⇒ else REJECT' (plan L208-210) fails bin/lib/dmc-v0.2.3-verify.sh V6 (:26 builds provider='' task; :56 expects ACCEPT). These are new FAILs in the pinned legacy aggregate the plan promises to keep EXACT (plan L28-29, L401-406) and the risk-row mitigation 'mock-001 still ACCEPT' (plan L425) is unachievable as designed. Must fix before approval: define compatibility semantics for the cross-check/floor (e.g. enforce provider equality only when task.provider_target.type is a real provider type and provider is non-empty, with the carve-out disclosed and negatively controlled) or obtain an explicit human-gated baseline re-pin — the plan currently forbids both outcomes simultaneously.

The plan's preserved-API constraint for the protected worker-result-check.py edit is factually incomplete against repo bytes: it pins only 'the module-level DISALLOWED name+shape' as the manual-import dynamic-import surface (plan L57-58, L182-183, risk row L429, citing manual-import-adapter.py:66-84), but manual-import-adapter.py:85 ALSO imports diff_paths ('diff_paths = _RC.diff_paths — unified-diff touched-path parser'). T012.1 rewrites diff_paths semantics and the T012.2 fidelity verb needs a richer parse (per-path hunk counts, entry kinds), inviting a signature/return-shape change that would break manual-import — a never-edit surface (master L92) — or silently alter its adapter-stage behavior that v0.3.3 C5a pins to 'adapter' for all five fixtures. Must fix before approval: add diff_paths(patch) -> set-of-path-strings (name, arity, return shape) to the explicit T012.1 preservation constraint, and state where the richer fidelity parse lives so it is not obtained by mutating diff_paths' contract.

advisories.statement: The reviewer_role example values in the committed contract do not all resolve in the registry the plan's review-check consults: worker-review.schema.md:17 illustrates 'critic|release-auditor', but orchestration/roles.json ids are critic-falsifier/release-auditor/verifier/etc. — `dmc-roles.py lookup critic` returns ROLES-UNKNOWN-ROLE. The design (registry lookup + may_mutate:false) is sound, but the plan should pin fixture reviewer_role values to real registry ids and note the schema-example divergence so a fixture authored from the schema text does not fail its own positive control.

The importlib precedent the plan mirrors includes `sys.dont_write_bytecode = True` at manual-import-adapter.py:33 — OUTSIDE the plan's cited :66-84 block. If the hardened validator/context-guard omit it, any invocation outside PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE-setting suites writes .claude/workers/providers/oauth-cli/__pycache__/ into the live repo, tripping the plan's own porcelain-unchanged acceptance and littering a never-edit tree. Name the guard explicitly in T012.1.

The new authorization artifact family is invisible to the host install surface the plan freezes: dmc-install.sh HARNESS_DIRS (:49) creates workers/{tasks,results,reviews,sessions} but not workers/authorizations, and the emitted host .gitignore block (:77-82) local-onlys every sibling worker artifact dir but not authorizations — while the plan forbids installer edits (plan L152, L164-165). Specify that `dmc worker authorize` creates its output directory (else it fails on fresh hosts) and record the host-policy divergence (authorizations not local-only on hosts) as a disclosed M9/M10 follow-up.

Two chain rules are under-specified for externally-authored records: (a) delegations.jsonl prev_hash = 'sha256(last existing line bytes)' does not state whether the terminating newline is included in the hashed bytes (plan L288-290) — record authors outside the module must know the exact rule; (b) the apply-authorization record includes a prev_hash field (plan L262-265) with no defined predecessor semantics anywhere (what a FIRST authorization chains to, and which verb verifies it) — the NEW schema file must define it or the field is dead weight that invites divergent readings.

Honest-scope wording: 'apply is FORBIDDEN without a PASSing apply-check (the machine gate...)' (plan L317-318) — the machine gate is a skill-invoked CLI; nothing in the Ring-1 hook path blocks an Edit/Write that is inside scope.lock but lacks an authorization, so the chain requirement is prose/skill-mandated per-host (the enforcement floor remains scope-lock adjudication). State this tier explicitly (one sentence in §5 or §Risks, matching the existing 'provenance-not-authentication' disclosure) so the M9 release gate and M10 enforcement matrix inherit the honest claim.

Diff-parsing residual: git does NOT c-quote paths that merely contain spaces, so the c-quote refusal (plan L201-203) does not cover ambiguous `diff --git a/X b/Y` / `Binary files a/X and b/Y differ` splits for space-bearing paths; header-split parsing there is heuristic. The dp != fc mismatch check keeps the failure mode fail-closed (over-reject, not bypass), but the plan should pin the parser's path-source precedence (---/+++/rename/copy lines authoritative; `diff --git` used for zero-path/binary detection) and/or add a space-path fixture row.

Scope disclosure for the human gate: the master Relevant-Files row for .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md (dmc-v1-runtime-upgrade.md:75) is milestone-tagged 'yes (M5)', and .harness/workers/authorizations/ has no master row at all — both edits rest on THIS plan's own gate approval rather than a master row tag. Precedented (M6.5/M8 did the same via their own gates) but worth an explicit line in the approval-record notes.
